## PAIRED BAR chart, EVIDENTIA style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-MAG-03",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "magnitude",
    "comparison"
  ],
  "form": "two horizontal bars per category on one shared scale",
  "data_required": "categories in a natural order with two comparable measures each",
  "family": "MAG",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-MAG-02 (vertical pairs for short labels)",
    "CH-DST-02 (dots when the gap is the story)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "the gap between the two measures is the story",
      "use": "CH-DST-02"
    },
    {
      "when": "labels are short and periods few",
      "use": "CH-MAG-02"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- two measures per category with long labels (plan vs actual, A vs B)
- natural category order that must be kept (process steps, stages)

Do NOT use when:
- ranking is the message (sort and use CH-RNK-01)
- before/after change (CH-DEV-02)
- more than 7 categories (pairs blur)

```json
  "unit": "on first bar label",
  "series_labels": "[name_a, name_b], printed on the first row",
  "categories": "array of {label, values: [a, b]}, input order kept"
}
```

### Rules
- input order kept: magnitude, not ranking
- series named on the first row labels, never a legend
- series_a muted, series_b primary
- shared zero-based scale for both measures

### Never do this
- legends
- two different scales
- sorting away a natural order
- more than two bars per category

### QA before delivering
- [ ] exactly two bars per row
- [ ] series named on first row
- [ ] unit once
- [ ] source present

### Fonts

- Title font: Georgia,serif, weight 700
- All other text: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 400 (600 on highlighted items)

### Language chrome

Draw the kicker bar and CAPS eyebrow defined in the layout, and use title y_with_eyebrow.
